title: Business Analyst
description: A Business Analyst in India typically spends their days analyzing business data, identifying trends, and providing insights to stakeholders. They work closely with cross-functional teams to develop and implement business solutions, often using data visualization tools and statistical models. Their primary goal is to drive business growth and improvement through data-driven decision making.

career_progression.year_0_2: Typically starts as a Junior Business Analyst, working under the guidance of a senior analyst. Focuses on developing data analysis and problem-solving skills.
career_progression.year_3_5: Moves to a Senior Business Analyst role, taking on more complex projects and leading small teams. Develops expertise in data visualization and statistical modeling.
career_progression.year_6_10: Promoted to a Business Analyst Manager, overseeing multiple projects and teams. Focuses on strategic planning and leadership development.
career_progression.entrepreneurship_exit: Can start a business as a consultant or by developing a data-driven product or service.
career_progression.global_pathway: Opportunities exist in the US, UK, and Australia for Business Analysts with advanced degrees and relevant experience.

india_social_context.first_year_reality: Starting salary is around ₹3-4 lakhs per annum, with a steep learning curve and long working hours.
common_misconceptions: Business Analysts are only data analysts
Business Analysts have no creative work
